html:
Why Payroll Software Pricing Matters for Jamaican Businesses

Running payroll in Jamaica is not simple. Between PAYE income tax, NIS contributions, NHT deductions, Education Tax, and Heart Trust/NTA levies, every pay cycle carries real compliance risk. Get it wrong and the Tax Administration Jamaica (TAJ) comes knocking — with interest and penalties that can dwarf whatever you thought you were saving by doing it manually.

For most Jamaican small business owners, the question about payroll software isn't really "which features do I need?" — it's "can I justify the cost?" That's a fair question. This guide gives you straight numbers, not vague "contact us for a quote" answers.

We'll cover what drives payroll software costs in Jamaica, realistic J$ pricing across different business sizes, what's included versus what costs extra, and whether the free options are actually worth your time. By the end you'll have everything you need to make a clear-headed decision.

What Drives the Cost of Payroll Software in Jamaica

Not all payroll software is priced the same way, and the differences matter. Here's what vendors actually use to set their prices:

Number of Employees

This is the biggest pricing lever across every platform. A bakery with 4 staff has vastly different needs — and costs — compared to a distribution company running 80 employees. Most platforms price per employee per month, which means costs scale predictably as you hire.

Compliance Updates

Jamaica's payroll regulations change. NIS rates, NHT thresholds, and PAYE brackets get adjusted. Good payroll software bakes compliance updates into the subscription so you're always calculating correctly. Cheaper or older tools often leave this to you — which is where mistakes happen.

Filing Features

Some platforms calculate the numbers but stop there. Others generate your TAJ monthly remittance schedules, P6 forms, NIS schedules, and year-end S01/S02 returns automatically. These features typically sit in higher tiers or cost extra.

HR Add-ons

Leave tracking, performance records, and employee self-service portals push prices up. If you only need payroll, make sure you're not paying for HR features you'll never use.

Support and Training

A Jamaican-focused support team that understands TAJ filing deadlines and NHT processes is genuinely valuable. Generic offshore support often costs less but creates more problems than it solves.

Payroll Software Pricing Tiers in Jamaica (2026)

Here are realistic price ranges in Jamaican dollars for payroll software in 2026, broken down by business size.

Small Business: 1–10 Employees

This is the sweet spot where payroll software delivers the clearest ROI. At this size, most owners are doing payroll themselves on a spreadsheet or paying an accountant per run.

  • Budget options: J$0–J$1,500/month (limited features, no compliance auto-updates, manual filing)
  • Mid-range: J$2,000–J$4,500/month (automated calculations, NIS/NHT/PAYE, basic reports)
  • Full-featured: J$4,500–J$8,000/month (TAJ filing prep, P6 generation, year-end returns, support)

PayrollJamaica starts at J$2,500/month for 1–5 employees, with full compliance calculations included from day one.

Medium Business: 11–50 Employees

At this scale, manual payroll is genuinely dangerous. The number of variables — different pay grades, varying overtime hours, statutory deductions, loan repayments — makes errors near-inevitable without proper software.

  • Budget options: J$5,000–J$12,000/month
  • Mid-range: J$12,000–J$25,000/month
  • Full-featured: J$25,000–J$45,000/month

At 25–50 employees, a single payroll error caught by TAJ can cost more than an entire year's software subscription.

Larger Business: 50+ Employees

Enterprise payroll in Jamaica typically involves multi-location support, department-level reporting, integration with accounting systems, and dedicated compliance support.

  • Typical range: J$45,000–J$150,000+/month
  • Most platforms at this tier quote custom pricing based on scope

What's Included — and What Costs Extra

This is where Jamaican business owners get caught out. A platform advertising "J$2,000/month payroll" might not include the features you actually need to stay compliant.

Usually Included in Base Price

  • PAYE (Pay As You Earn) income tax calculations
  • NIS (National Insurance Scheme) employee and employer contributions
  • NHT (National Housing Trust) deductions
  • Education Tax calculations
  • Heart Trust/NTA levy
  • Basic payslip generation
  • Direct bank transfer files (for most major Jamaican banks)

Often Charged as Add-ons

  • TAJ monthly remittance filing prep — generating the S01 schedule for electronic filing
  • P6 forms — the annual employee tax certificates required by TAJ
  • Year-end returns (S02) — employer annual returns to TAJ
  • NIS annual reconciliation reports
  • NHT annual contribution schedules
  • Multi-company support (if you own several businesses)
  • Accountant/auditor access portal
  • Historical data migration

Before signing up for any platform, ask specifically: "Does your base plan generate the TAJ S01 filing schedule and P6 forms?" If they hesitate or redirect you to a higher tier, factor that into your real cost.

The Real Cost of NOT Using Payroll Software

Many Jamaican business owners focus on the cost of the software and miss the much larger cost of not having it. Let's put numbers on both sides of that equation.

Your Time

A typical payroll run for 10 employees — calculating PAYE, NIS, NHT, Education Tax, processing varying hours, preparing pay advices — takes 3–6 hours when done manually in a spreadsheet. At even a modest J$3,000/hour value of your time, that's J$9,000–J$18,000 per pay cycle, or J$18,000–J$36,000 per month for bi-weekly payroll. Software that costs J$3,500/month is paying for itself in the first hour.

Accountant Fees

Outsourcing payroll to an accountant in Jamaica typically costs J$8,000–J$25,000 per month for a small business, depending on complexity and frequency. Many accountants charge extra for year-end P6 preparation and TAJ filing support. That's J$96,000–J$300,000 annually before any extras.

TAJ Penalties

Late or incorrect PAYE remittances attract penalties of 50% of the tax due plus interest at 16.5% per annum. A J$150,000 monthly PAYE obligation paid even one month late costs J$75,000 in penalties alone. One mistake can exceed an entire year of software costs.

NIS and NHT Penalties

Both the National Insurance Scheme and the National Housing Trust impose late payment surcharges and interest. Repeat non-compliance can result in legal action and director liability.

Audit Risk

Businesses with sloppy payroll records are more likely to be flagged during TAJ audits. The cost of an audit — in accountant fees, lost time, and potential back-taxes — routinely runs into the millions of dollars.

Free vs Paid Payroll Software in Jamaica — What Free Gets You

"Free payroll software Jamaica" is a search people make, and there are some free or freemium options out there. Here's an honest look at what you actually get.

What Free Typically Offers

  • Basic gross-to-net pay calculation
  • Simple payslip templates
  • Limited employee count (usually 1–3)
  • No automatic compliance updates when rates change
  • No TAJ filing integration
  • No P6 generation
  • Limited or no support

What Free Doesn't Get You

Free tools are usually built for one market and adapted loosely for others. Jamaica-specific requirements — the Heart Trust/NTA levy split, the NHT employer contribution rate, TAJ filing formats — are often wrong, outdated, or simply missing. You end up doing the compliance work yourself anyway, which defeats the purpose.

A 14-day free trial of a proper Jamaican payroll platform is genuinely more useful than a perpetually "free" tool that requires constant manual correction. PayrollJamaica offers a free 14-day trial — no credit card required — so you can verify everything works correctly for your specific business before spending a cent.

How PayrollJamaica Pricing Compares to the Alternatives

Here's a direct cost comparison for a Jamaican business with 10 employees running bi-weekly payroll:

Method Monthly Cost (J$) Annual Cost (J$) Compliance Risk Time Required
DIY Spreadsheet J$0 (software) + J$18,000–36,000 (your time) J$216,000–J$432,000 in time cost High — manual errors, outdated rates 6–12 hrs/month
Outsourced Accountant J$12,000–J$25,000 J$144,000–J$300,000 Low–Medium (depends on accountant) 1–2 hrs/month (coordination)
PayrollJamaica J$4,500–J$7,500 J$54,000–J$90,000 Low — auto-updated compliance Under 1 hr/month

Time cost estimate based on J$3,000/hour opportunity cost. Accountant fees are estimates for Greater Kingston area; rates vary.

The comparison isn't really about software cost. It's about total cost of ownership — including your time, the risk premium on compliance errors, and the penalty exposure you carry when running payroll manually.

Want to calculate your exact savings? Use our free payroll cost calculator to see what payroll is actually costing your business right now.

Tips for Choosing the Right Pricing Tier

Before you sign up for any payroll software in Jamaica, run through this checklist:

  1. Count your active employees — not just permanent staff. Casual workers, contractors, and part-time staff often need to be included if they receive taxable income.
  2. Confirm TAJ filing is included — specifically the S01 monthly remittance schedule and annual S02 employer return.
  3. Ask about P6 generation — these are due to employees by March 31 each year. If your software doesn't generate them, add that cost to your annual estimate.
  4. Check for bank integration — most Jamaican banks accept payroll files in specific formats. Confirm your bank is supported.
  5. Verify compliance update policy — when NIS rates change or PAYE brackets are adjusted, does the software update automatically?
  6. Test support responsiveness — send a question during the trial period. How long does it take to get a useful answer?

Frequently Asked Questions: Payroll Software Pricing in Jamaica

How much does payroll software cost in Jamaica?

For a small business with 1–10 employees, expect to pay between J$2,000 and J$8,000 per month for a proper, compliance-ready platform. Mid-sized businesses (11–50 employees) typically pay J$12,000–J$45,000/month. Prices vary based on features, filing support, and the number of employees.

Is there free payroll software for Jamaican businesses?

There are free or freemium tools, but most lack Jamaica-specific compliance features — particularly TAJ filing formats, correct Heart Trust/NTA rates, and P6 generation. A free trial of a full-featured platform (like PayrollJamaica's 14-day free trial) is a better option than a permanently "free" tool that requires manual correction.

Does payroll software include TAJ filing in Jamaica?

Not always. Some platforms include TAJ monthly remittance schedule generation in their base price; others treat it as an add-on. Always confirm before subscribing. PayrollJamaica includes TAJ filing prep, NIS schedules, NHT reporting, and P6 generation in the standard plan.

What's the cheapest way to run payroll in Jamaica compliantly?

PayrollJamaica at J$2,500/month for 1–5 employees is one of the most affordable fully-compliant options in the market. When you factor in your time saved and penalty risk avoided, it's cheaper than any DIY approach for virtually all Jamaican businesses.

Is it worth paying for payroll software if I only have 3 employees?

Yes. Even at 3 employees, manual payroll takes 2–4 hours per pay cycle when done properly. At J$2,500/month, PayrollJamaica pays for itself in the first hour of time saved. And it eliminates the risk of a TAJ penalty that could cost J$50,000–J$100,000+ from a single filing error.

Do Jamaican payroll software prices include NIS and NHT compliance?

At reputable platforms, yes. NIS (National Insurance Scheme) and NHT (National Housing Trust) calculations should be automatically updated when government-mandated rates change. Always confirm this is included in the base subscription, not an add-on tier.

Can I switch payroll software providers in Jamaica without losing data?

Most platforms support data export. The key items you'll want to transfer are year-to-date PAYE, NIS, NHT, and Education Tax totals for each employee — critical for mid-year switches so your cumulative deductions remain accurate. PayrollJamaica's team assists with data migration as part of onboarding.

Bottom Line: What Should You Pay?

The right payroll software for your Jamaican business isn't necessarily the cheapest — it's the one that keeps you fully compliant with TAJ, NIS, and NHT at a cost that makes financial sense for your size.

For most small businesses (under 15 employees), the math is clear: J$2,500–J$5,500/month for a fully compliant platform is dramatically cheaper than the combination of your time, accountant fees, and penalty exposure from doing it manually or with inadequate tools.

Payroll software pricing in Jamaica has become more competitive and transparent in 2026. You should not be paying for features you don't need, and you should not be accepting "contact us for pricing" from a vendor for a small-business subscription.
